applicationWindow.notes: Tule elk are unique to California; Roosevelt elk in northwest zones. Very few NR tags issued — residents and NR compete equally in most zones.

nr.notes: Total NR cost when drawn: ~$1,535 (NR elk tag ~$1,487 + $48 NR hunting license).

pointSystem.notes: Premium zones require many points. NR quota ~10%.

applicationWindow.notes: Zone X and most premium zones require points. A-zone is general with OTC for residents but draw for NR.

nr.notes: Total NR cost when drawn: ~$348 (NR deer tag ~$300 + $48 NR hunting license). Zones A and X most coveted.

pointSystem.notes: Preference points by zone. NR must draw most zones.

otc.notes: A-zone is general/OTC for residents but draw-only for NR.

applicationWindow.notes: Northeastern CA (Modoc / Lassen counties). Underrated opportunity but residents have better odds.

nr.notes: Total NR cost when drawn: ~$348 (NR pronghorn tag ~$300 + $48 NR hunting license).

applicationWindow.notes: Desert bighorn in southern CA. Sierra Nevada bighorn extremely rare — typically a 15–25 year commitment.

nr.notes: Total NR cost when drawn: ~$1,548 (NR bighorn sheep tag ~$1,500 + $48 NR hunting license). Apply annually for points.

applicationWindow.notes: OTC purchase window. State-wide quota applies — season closes when quota is reached, sometimes mid-season. Check current quota status at wildlife.ca.gov before purchasing.

nr.notes: NR tag ~$282 + $16 NR license = ~$298 total. Sierra Nevada and Coast Ranges hold large bear populations.

otc.notes: OTC for NR with state-wide quota. Season can close mid-year when the quota is reached — verify current status before buying.
